name: Qubes OS
tagline: Security through compartmentalisation — each task in its own VM
description: Qubes OS is a security-focused desktop OS that implements isolation through compartmentalisation: every application, workflow, or trust domain runs in a separate lightweight Xen virtual machine called a qube. Your banking browser, your work email, and your personal files each live in separate qubes that cannot see each other. Network and USB access are handled by dedicated VMs, and a disposable qube can be spun up for untrusted content and discarded when done. Endorsed by Edward Snowden and used by high-risk individuals worldwide, Qubes requires significant RAM and CPU resources and a learning curve, but provides a level of security impossible on a conventional OS.
